profiles.description: We research and invest in companies developing expansion technologies shaping the future of humanity, including space, AI, robotics, and advanced infrastructure

tweets.text: 2,000 beams is the interesting number. At any given moment 10,000 sats 25-35 sats a visible to a user terminal. That means being able to serve ~50,000-70,000 customers with 500Mbps within view of any given terminal, once V3 hits 10,000 sats in orbit, and 10x that when they hit their ultimate target of 100,000.
Shorting a literal rocketship sounds exactly like what you're not supposed to do…
SpaceX should require all analyst requesting attendance on their quarterly earnings calls to prove they watched the most recent flight test
How long until SpaceX needs a dedicated launch account with constant live streams and launch updates?

MTS did a good job of explaining our analyst speak in everyday language. A skill I don't really have. 

For context Elon's reveal of AI1 sats being scoped for Rubin cuts into our worst case orbital tax scenario nearly in half immediately. Or model suggested this would happen in AI2. 

Bottomline: single node inference is happening and more efficient than you'd assume. 

Btw- Google suncatcher solving this problem with formation flying is a valid way to tackle the problem.

If you're an investor, you should definitely be watching this!

What I'm paying closest attention to…

- booster raptor relights during decent
- starship raptor burn and relight
- V3 actual Starlinks

Also there are normally a bunch of good business updates during these streams.

If you're a trader save your time there is very little value for you arbitrage here.
Incredible cadence. Raptors relights on descent. Functional V3 starlinks. Important one to watch.

Missed this first time around. 20 real Starlink V3 sats. Will deploy solar arrays and antennas and attempt ground station connection. Very big test toward functional Starlink V3 deployments by ~EoY. 

First functional launch is significant inflection in Starlink V3 ramp which is equivalent to global bandwidth capacity ramp. 

Very good to see real sats on board. Hopefully we get to see them deployed from pez as well!
Big week. First launch test in public markets. I wonder if the overreaction will be positive or negative?

Early snapshot into the value of AI bundling. 

Extend this to Starlink + Inference. Supergrok monthly included with 500Mbps internet  is going to be a very very hard AI consumer bundle to compete against for either the labs or ISPs. 

Add in mobile and X money perks. 

Paying for a chatbot is not obvious to most consumers. Wrap internet in and it won't even be a question.

The only thing stopping them is capacity and terminals and that changes in 2027. 

The competition will be forced to partner across the stack.
Co-designed models for orbital data centers will become normalized in less than 18 months.

SpaceXAI the model factory.
Speed to deployment as an advantage will look obvious in a few months, currently only talked about by the team building it.
Just wait until Intelligence/$ goes orbital

Phenomenal breakdown of Starlink shells. As they go lower it certainly appears they are pushing the limits of existing satellites design for 5 years life times in VLEO orbits. 

It's SpaceX style to challenge the accepted truth. In this case VLEO is expected to require much more exotic technology and propulsion to maintain the 5 year life. Air breathing and all that. 

Is it possible Starlink existing propulsion and structures are capable of surviving long enough (maybe less than 5 years but not so much less it kills economics)? 

If your sat costs and launch cost are low enough then a 3-4 year life cycle is worth the increased performance and you can use your deorbit propellant for a little bit more station keeping.

After you're done watching the SpaceX doc bookmark to watch our M33 pod. We talked with @peterrhague this week to zoom out and discuss a little bit about colonizing the solar system. And a little bit about the realism of our Martian office design. 

Incredibly refreshing conversation with space optimism.
Seems fairly significant. Looking forward to incorporating into our research work.
Where does the case for space start? The Sun's energy that misses Earth outweighs what hits us ~2 billion to one. Capture a thousandth of it and 16 trillion people live like billionaires.

Incredible Mach33 podcast this week with special guest, pro-solar system colonization, optimistic astrophysics PhD @peterrhague  on Orbital Refueling, the Case for Mars, and a C Rewrite That Shrinks Grok's Release Cycle.

Make sure to check out Peter's Writing  at

Given all the surprise around the Starlink Mobile rumors it's pretty clear many investors are modeling Starlink incorrectly.
To help get analysts up to speed, i've opened up all of our Starlink Mobile related research for public viewing for a limited time.
we've published 7 highly relevant articles on starlink and starlink mobile in the last 10 months.
If you'd read these you would not be surprised...
Starlink Mobile bundling unit economics (May 19):
https://t.co/e5Rd8nbDdc
7x power increase from new FCC ruling (May 6th):
https://t.co/L6GndLxhnb
What End to End Connectivity Means (Jan 28th): 
https://t.co/3jnePTRLsR
Starlink's Deflationary Path (Sept 17th):
https://t.co/5EYdX0qILz
Echostar Deal - Full Stack Connectivity path (Sept 10):
https://t.co/0SEVIfUONx
Starship reduce bandwidth launch cost by 50x (Aug 27th) :
https://t.co/I1ixVm7jrN
Fiber vs Starlink (Aug 13th): 
https://t.co/R6x3H1F5iG
I hope this helps professional analysts and individual investors understand why our SpaceX Gigamodel has such a strong Starlink revenue projection.

Umm that's we call a very big learning rate sir.
For context, Mach33 models suggest 50x-100x bandwidth increase over the next 5 years.
Certainly feels like grok and perplexity's core product values are a match made in heaven.
Latency and ISL impacts on inference network coherence are a very interesting topic we're publishing analysis on this week.

It really is shocking how few people care enough about the 1-5 year time horizon to even try sharpening the skill. Hardware and deep technologies lend themselves well to forecasting truth over that time horizon. Market forces change wildly over that time frame physics and engineering not so much.

Yes, Jim and Daniel, current version works in suburbs (I use it daily in 250+ person/km2) that's beyond rural. 

Denser suburbs are next with V3 (up to 1,000/km2), its nonsense to say the physics aren't there they absolutely are there. 

Current market penetration isn't evenly distributed it's why you see some markets at higher cost than others.

As capacity + terminals scale and costs come down it will be ubiquitous everywhere outside the larger metros. 

You'll be able to drive through US neighborhoods and count dishes to estimate regional market penetration. 

Dense city physics is far far easy to solve economically than rural broadband was. Just a matter of pick the preferred method.
